seo_description: Current local time in Toronto, Canada. EST (UTC-5) in winter, EDT (UTC-4) in summer. Live clock, TSX hours, and Toronto time difference to London, LA, and other cities.

faq.question: What time is it in Toronto right now?
faq.answer: Toronto, Ontario, Canada uses Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) in winter and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) during Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is America/Toronto. Toronto operates on Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5) from early November to mid-March, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) from mid-March to early November. Toronto follows the same DST schedule as New York City, meaning both cities are always at the same time. The live clock above shows the current Toronto time updated in real time.

faq.question: What time zone is Toronto in?
faq.answer: Toronto is in the Eastern Time Zone, using Eastern Standard Time (EST/UTC-5) in winter and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT/UTC-4) in summer. Toronto shares this time zone with New York City, Boston, Miami, Atlanta, and most of the US East Coast. Ontario, where Toronto is located, observes the same DST transitions as the US — the second Sunday of March and the first Sunday of November.

faq.question: Does Toronto observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: Yes. Toronto (and all of Ontario) springs clocks forward 1 hour to EDT (UTC-4) on the second Sunday of March, and falls back to EST (UTC-5) on the first Sunday of November. This mirrors the US daylight saving schedule exactly, keeping Toronto permanently synchronized with New York City year-round. Canada has discussed eliminating DST nationally but no change has been implemented.

faq.question: Is Toronto time the same as New York time?
faq.answer: Yes. Toronto and New York City are always at the same time. Both are in the Eastern Time Zone and observe daylight saving time on the same dates. This means that business calls, meetings, and broadcasts between the two cities never require time zone conversion — a practical advantage for the significant economic ties between Canada's largest city and the US financial capital.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Toronto and London?
faq.answer: Toronto is 5 hours behind London during Eastern Standard Time (November–March) and 4 hours behind during Eastern Daylight Time (March–November). When it is 9:00 AM in Toronto (EST), it is 2:00 PM in London. The best window for Toronto–London calls is 9:00 AM–1:00 PM Toronto time, catching London before end of business at 5:00–6:00 PM GMT.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Toronto and Los Angeles?
faq.answer: Toronto is consistently 3 hours ahead of Los Angeles year-round. Both cities observe daylight saving time on the same schedule, maintaining a constant 3-hour gap. When it is 9:00 AM in Los Angeles, it is 12:00 PM noon in Toronto. This consistent difference simplifies Canada-wide east–west scheduling.

faq.question: What time does the Toronto Stock Exchange open?
faq.answer: The Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX) opens at 9:30 AM EST/EDT and closes at 4:00 PM EST/EDT, Monday through Friday. Pre-market trading runs from 7:00 AM. The TSX is North America's third-largest stock exchange and the world's ninth-largest, listing over 1,500 companies with a combined market capitalisation exceeding CAD $3 trillion. Its hours are identical to the NYSE.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Toronto from Europe?
faq.answer: From London (GMT/BST), the best time to call Toronto is between 2:00 PM and 6:00 PM London time (9:00 AM–1:00 PM Toronto EST/EDT). This window covers Toronto's core morning business hours while still being within UK business hours. From Paris or Berlin (CET in winter), call between 3:00 PM and 7:00 PM local time to reach Toronto in the morning.
